text: We investigate the intrinsic carrier mobility (μ) in monolayer MoS2 sheets and nanoribbons by using first-principle calculations combined with deformation potential theory. We observe a prominent feature of mobility oscillations with width, which still maintains its major component in a comparable level with the 2D sheet for armchair MoS2 nanoribbons. Moreover, there is also a reversal of transport polarity at room temperature, while the 2D MoS2 sheet always behaves as a p-type material with a large hole mobility of 200.52 cm2 V-1 s-1 and a small electron mobility of 72.16 cm2 V-1 s-1, a 4 nm wide armchair nanoribbon is transformed into an n-type material at room temperature with a hole mobility of 49.72 cm2 V-1 s-1 and an electron mobility of 190.89 cm2 V-1 s-1. Our findings also indicate that the width confinement combined with adequate edge engineering would have a promising future in tailoring the carrier transport behavior in MoS2-based materials.
